specs.section_path: 15-Layer Summary Table > 3.2 The Dynamic Spine: The Unified Phase Dynamics Equation (UPDE) > Quasicriticality and Self-Organisation > A The Universal Dynamic Regime: Quasicriticality > Functional Implications:
source_formulae: P0R02863: Facilitates the transmission of weak signals across scales via long-range temporal correlations (LRTC), enabling efficient bidirectional causality.
P0R02864: The system maintains this state via self-organised criticality (SOC) mechanisms at each layer, adjusting the local branching parameter (sigmaL) towards unity:
P0R02866: Where L is the homeostatic relaxation rate (e.g., synaptic plasticity L4, cultural adaptation L11).

P0R02867: The homeostatic relaxation described by L is not an abstract controller but is grounded in concrete biophysical mechanisms. At the level of neural tissue (L4), this self-tuning to criticality is implemented by homeostatic synaptic plasticity. Local rules that scale synaptic weights to maintain a target firing rate are sufficient to guide the network's global dynamics to the critical point where sigma1.

P0R02868: This mechanism, supported by extensive experimental and theoretical work, provides a robust and biologically plausible basis for the emergence and maintenance of the quasicritical regime, replacing fine-tuning with adaptive self-organisation.
